title: I can't comment on my videos

enrichment.summary: Kyle explains that he has been unable to comment on roughly 9% of his own videos since he started uploading, suspects YouTube has restricted his commenting ability as a penalty (possibly due to his being a stoner or a prior incident in 2011 where he got drunk and posted explicit videos that YouTube deleted), and misses the interaction since commenting was one of his favorite things about the platform.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: One of his favorite things about YouTube; genuinely misses the interaction
Rarely does it — only after someone relentlessly posts long attacks across multiple videos
Suspects it's retaliation or a penalty, possibly tied to his 2011 explicit video or his status as a stoner, but has no confirmation
Hasn't bothered and doesn't plan to, though the issue is starting to annoy him

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm able to comment on maybe 9% of my videos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been unable to comment on my videos since I started uploading.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't know why I can't comment on my videos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't contacted YouTube about the commenting issue.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I probably won't contact YouTube about the commenting issue.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Not being able to comment on my videos will likely start to bother me.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Commenting on my videos was one of my favorite things to do.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I always commented on the news.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I never blocked people unless they repeatedly attacked me over multiple videos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I love having conversations with people in the comments.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can't comment on people's comments anymore, which is probably a big reason I don't engage as much.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: YouTube may have diminished my commenting ability because I'm a stoner.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: In 2011, I got really drunk and made a video where I exposed myself.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: YouTube deleted the video immediately.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was shocked when I realized what I had done the next morning.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: A cop confronted me about my behavior that night.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was using public Wi-Fi at the time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: YouTube disabled my ability to upload comments for two weeks after that incident.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think YouTube has permanently disabled my ability to comment on my own videos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I never received an email from YouTube explaining why my commenting was disabled.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There might be a law or policy that led to my commenting being disabled.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might have received enough complaints to trigger the restriction.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I will show what the commenting restriction looks like to prove I'm not lying.

transcript.text: I'm completely unable to comment on any of my videos. Pretty much. 99% of my videos, 95% of my videos I'm unable to comment on. This has been going on since I started uploading videos. I don't know what's going on. I haven't written on YouTube. I don't have the time for that. I don't think I will.

But it probably will start to bother me because commenting on my videos was my very favorite thing. One of my favorite things. I always commented on videos. I never blocked people. I have a pretty good block policy. I'll only block somebody if all they do is attack me in really long things as though they're trying to have a conversation with me and I'm ignoring them.

You know, like people normally do on YouTube. And then I respond to them and I try to have a conversation with them and then they don't respond to me. And then the very next video, they'll do a really long thing insulting me as though they're trying to talk to me and I'm not trying to talk to them. And I do it over and over again. Like for two or three weeks straight. That's when I'll block somebody. But otherwise, I don't block people. I love commenting on people. Commenting. I love having full-on carrying conversations. People don't comment on me anymore.

But probably a big reason is because I wasn't coming to them. But I can't. And so I don't know what's going on. Maybe YouTube has diminished my... I've become some kind of a second-class citizen on YouTube because I'm a stoner. That would not surprise me at all.
But back at the beginning of 2011, I did a video. I got really drunk. I was in my van. I got really drunk and I did a video where I got my dick out. Three videos. I did three videos where I did that.
And YouTube deleted the video like right away. It was like midnight. I got really drunk. And I did that. And then I fell asleep. And I got up at like 10. And I was like, do I really do that?
This cop actually woke me up. He's like, what are you doing here? I'm like, oh, huh, oh.
And then, because I was using somebody's Wi-Fi in front of their house. I think they probably called a cop. Because I was like screaming and stuff.
But I got up. And I was like, did I really do that video? And I checked and it had already been deleted.
But they disabled me from being able to upload any comments or to comment on any of my videos for two weeks.
I think it was the same thing. It would say comment failed to post if you were to try.
So, I think it's the same thing. I think YouTube has disabled my ability to comment on my own videos.
Maybe, I don't know why. Maybe there's some law.
I never got an email saying that they did that to me.
But maybe like if you're a stoner or I don't know. Maybe I got enough complaints.
But I don't know. But I will show you. I'll show you exactly what it looks like so you don't think I'm lying.
